title: TV Show Episode Not Shown Because of DJ's Problem
summary: A TV channel called Channel 4 decided not to show a special episode of a baking show called The Great British Bake Off. This episode had a famous DJ named Scott Mills in it. Scott Mills used to work for the BBC radio, but he was fired because of a serious accusation from the past. The police looked into it before and did not charge him with a crime. Channel 4 said the accusation is very serious, so they think it is not right to air the episode now. They will show a different episode instead. Scott Mills was also supposed to be in a concert with a band called Boyzone, but he is not on the list anymore. He also stopped being an ambassador for a charity that helps people with a disease called multiple sclerosis. The charity said he was important to them for over 10 years, but he is stepping back while they check the situation.

keywords.explanation: a company that makes and shows TV programs
a TV program where people bake cakes and other treats
a person who plays music on the radio or at events
when someone says another person did something wrong
an organization that helps people in need

Who was in the episode that was not shown?
Why did Channel 4 decide not to air the episode?
What will Channel 4 show instead of the episode with Scott Mills?
What else did Scott Mills stop doing?
How long was Scott Mills an ambassador for the charity?

background_read: This article is about a TV decision related to a celebrity. Scott Mills is a DJ who was accused of something serious from the past. Even though the police did not charge him, Channel 4 thinks it is not appropriate to show him on TV now. This shows how companies sometimes make choices based on public feelings and accusations. It also affects other events like concerts and charity work.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scott Mills, a DJ, and Channel 4, a TV company
WHAT: Channel 4 will not broadcast a Bake Off episode with Scott Mills due to a serious accusation
WHERE: In the UK, on TV and at events like concerts
WHY: Because the accusation is serious, and Channel 4 believes it is not appropriate to air the episode now

perspectives.description: They think it is not right to show the episode because of the serious accusation against Scott Mills.
